name: Astrum
oneLine: Solo-run space documentary channel that breaks down solar system science and probe missions using real imagery.
bio: Alex McColgan launched Astrum in 2013 as a faceless channel dedicated to making space science accessible, with a self-imposed rule of using only real images from probes and telescopes wherever possible. Episodes are weekly long-form video essays on celestial bodies, missions, and astronomical phenomena. The brand has expanded into the Astrum Space and Sleep Space podcasts and a sister channel Astrum Earth.
style: narrated space documentaries built around real probe and telescope imagery, weekly long-form
notable: deep-dive series on Saturn's moons and outer planets
